bills.title: Inaction Has Consequences Act
bills.stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on House Administration.
bills.plain_summary: This bill says that members of Congress will not get their paychecks if their part of Congress does not pass all the yearly spending bills on time. The House and Senate each have to pass these bills before October 1st, which is when the government's budget year starts. If they don't finish the work on time, their pay gets put aside and they can't have it until they pass all the bills or until their time in Congress ends. This rule would start in 2026 and is meant to make Congress members work harder to avoid government shutdowns.
subjects: Government employee pay, benefits, personnel management
bills.subjects_str: Appropriations, Budget process, Executive agency funding and structure, Government employee pay, benefits, personnel management, Legislative rules and procedure, Members of Congress
bills.plain_keywords_str: congress pay, government shutdown, spending bills, congressional salary, budget deadline, october 1st, house senate pay, government funding, congress paycheck, lawmakers salary, federal budget, government money

bills.title: Protecting our Communities from Sexual Predators Act
bills.short_title: Protecting our Communities from Sexual Predators Act
bills.stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
bills.plain_summary: This bill makes new rules about non-citizens who commit sexual assault crimes in America. If a non-citizen is arrested or charged with sexual assault, they must be held in jail and cannot be released. Non-citizens who are convicted of sexual assault cannot enter the United States and must be removed from the country if they are already here. The bill is meant to protect communities by making sure sexual predators who are not American citizens are kept away from the public.
bills.subjects_str: Border security and unlawful immigration, Criminal investigation, prosecution, interrogation, Detention of persons, Immigration status and procedures, Sex offenses
bills.plain_keywords_str: immigration sex crimes, deport sexual predators, jail immigrant rapists, sexual assault deportation, non-citizen sex offenders, immigrant detention sexual assault, remove foreign criminals, sexual predator immigration, border security sex crimes, deport convicted rapists, immigrant sexual assault laws, foreign sexual predators removal

bills.title: Proposing an amendment to the Constitution of the United States to require that the Supreme Court of the United States be composed of nine justices.
bills.stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
bills.plain_summary: This bill wants to change the Constitution to make sure the Supreme Court always has exactly nine judges. Right now, the Constitution doesn't say how many judges the Supreme Court must have, so Congress could change that number if they wanted to. The bill says there should be one Chief Justice and eight other justices, and that number could never be changed. To make this part of the Constitution, two thirds of Congress and three fourths of all the states would have to agree to it.
bills.subjects_str: Constitution and constitutional amendments, Judges, Supreme Court
bills.plain_keywords_str: supreme court size, nine justices, court packing, supreme court judges, constitutional amendment, fix supreme court number, limit supreme court justices, supreme court reform, nine judge limit, court expansion ban

bills.title: Tennessee Valley Authority Salary Transparency Act
bills.short_title: Tennessee Valley Authority Salary Transparency Act
bills.stage_label: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Environment and Public Works.
bills.plain_summary: This bill makes the Tennessee Valley Authority report more information to Congress each year. The Tennessee Valley Authority is a government company that provides electricity to Tennessee and nearby states. Starting again, they must file a yearly financial report with Congress every March. The report must include the names, salaries, and job duties of management workers who earn more than a certain government pay level, but this salary information stays private and is not shared with the public.
subjects: Government employee pay, benefits, personnel management
bills.subjects_str: Government employee pay, benefits, personnel management, Government information and archives, Personnel records, Tennessee Valley Authority
bills.plain_keywords_str: TVA salaries, Tennessee Valley Authority pay, government employee salaries, electricity company wages, Tennessee power company, executive compensation, management salaries, government transparency, public utility pay, congressional reporting

bills.title: High Rise Fire Sprinkler Incentive Act of 2025
bills.short_title: High Rise Fire Sprinkler Incentive Act of 2025
bills.stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.
bills.plain_summary: This bill helps building owners who add fire sprinkler systems to tall apartment buildings. It lets them save money on their taxes faster than before. Instead of taking many years to get the tax break, building owners can now get it over just fifteen years. This makes it cheaper for them to install sprinklers, which helps keep people safer in tall buildings.
bills.subjects_str: Business investment and capital, Fires, Income tax deductions, Residential rehabilitation and home repair
bills.plain_keywords_str: fire sprinklers, apartment building safety, high rise buildings, tax breaks for landlords, building fire safety, sprinkler system installation, tall building fire protection, tax incentives for safety upgrades, apartment fire prevention, building owner tax savings, fire safety equipment, residential building improvements
